spec_match.rationale: Page demonstrates the headline Expose 'onanimationcancel' event to GlobalEventHandlers surface (CSS, HTML, IDL); residual gaps in spec vocabulary: css-animations, interface-globaleventhandlers.

interactive.rationale: Live UI is real (button); controls include "Show info toast"; "Show urgent (replaces existing)"; "Yank: display:none mid-animation" — well past the no-static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature-index blurb and page H1/lede share the same framing and surface the same API affordances.

edge_cases.rationale: One failure-mode signal is present but most edge cases (errors, unsupported environment, empty/zero inputs) are not demonstrated.

references.rationale: The concept page now includes the required canonical ChromeStatus self-heal link https://chromestatus.com/feature/5160464445210624.

design_system.rationale: Uses CSS variables, no raw hex in <style>, button overrides set background and color together, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Show the failure modes on cancel-aware-toast
openQuestions.detail: Right now the page only demonstrates the happy path. Add at least one of: a feature-detection probe with graceful fallback, an error/throw path (empty input, invalid argument), and a boundary case (zero, max, abort).

summary: cancel-aware-toast covers the happy path of Expose 'onanimationcancel' event to GlobalEventHandlers and is interactive enough to clear the bar, but spec coverage and edge cases need filling in — see open questions. ChromeStatus reference link resolved.
